title: Guard BridgeClient.ready against an unhandled-rejection crash when validatePaths rejects before the first call
evidence: Constructor does `this.ready = this.validatePaths();` with no rejection handler attached until the first `call()` awaits it. In child-extension.ts the BridgeClient is constructed at extension init, but the first `call()` only happens when a gated tool fires — potentially much later. If validatePaths rejects in the interim (realpath failure, `taskkill is unavailable` on win32, executable identity invalid), the rejection is unhandled at the end of the microtask turn; on modern Node (default --unhandled-rejections=throw) that crashes the child/parent process instead of degrading through the fail-direction bridge response.
impact: A recoverable path-validation failure becomes a hard process crash of the Pi extension host or isolated child rather than the designed warn/block degradation.
recommendation: Attach a no-op catch to `this.ready` in the constructor (keeping the original promise for call() to await), or defer validatePaths to the first call().
acceptance_criteria: A BridgeClient whose validatePaths rejects with no call() issued produces no unhandledRejection
